26 February 2026 Military News

Operations

News Reports

Current Operations

  • Dem Leadership, Smith, Meeks, Himes, Khanna Announce Iran WPR Vote Next Week House Armed Services Committee - Democrats 26 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Representatives Adam Smith, Ranking Member of the House Armed Services Committee; House Democratic Leader Hakeem Jeffries; Democratic Whip Katherine Clark; Democratic Caucus Chair Pete Aguilar; Gregory W. Meeks, Ranking Member of the House Foreign Affairs Committee; Jim Himes, Ranking Member of the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence; and Ro Khanna, Ranking Member of the House Select Committee on the Strategic Competition Between the United States and the Chinese Communist Party, today announced that House Democrats will force a vote on the Khanna-Massie Iran War Powers Resolution next week:

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Trump delivers State of Union address amid affordability concerns, rising polarization People's Daily 26 Feb 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday night delivered his first State of the Union address of his second presidential term, seeking to project confidence in the nation's economic trajectory against a backdrop of sliding approval ratings and mounting midterm anxiety.

  • Acquisition Reform Means a Focus on Warfighter Success DoD News 26 Feb 2026 -- The War Department aims to modernize how acquisition — the buying of weapons and other materiel from manufacturers — is done to ensure American warfighters get the best tools, at the right price, and as quickly as possible.
  • Joint Force Refines CBRN Defense and Response Capabilities at JBLM Army News 26 Feb 2026 -- To ensure the Joint Force can fight and win in any environment, key U.S. Army and organizations from across the Department of War have gathered at Joint Base Lewis-McChord to refine the complex challenge of operating through a chemical or biological attack.
  • Air Force leaders discuss priorities for the Air Force's Future Force Design US Air Force 26 Feb 2026 -- Senior Air Force leaders and industry experts discussed the priorities for Air Force's Future Force Design during a panel at the Air and Space Forces Association's 2026 Warfare Symposium in Aurora, Feb. 25.
  • Healy highlights Reserve advantage during AFA symposium US Air Force 26 Feb 2026 -- The commander of Air Force Reserve Command told Airmen and Guardians attending the Air and Space Forces Association's 2026 Warfare Symposium that Reserve Airman are an advantage to the Department of the Air Force.
  • Acquisition leaders detail Warfighting Acquisition Strategy at AFA symposium US Air Force 26 Feb 2026 -- Top acquisition leaders for the Department of the Air Force detailed how the acquisition process is being reshaped to meet the demands of modern warfare, during a panel at the Air and Space Forces Association's 2026 Warfare Symposium, Feb. 25.
  • AMC interim commander emphasizes Airmen, aircraft readiness importance at AFA US Air Force 26 Feb 2026 -- Lt. Gen. Rebecca Sonkiss, who took over as Air Mobility Command's interim commander earlier this month, addressed servicemembers, industry and journalists on the state of mobility readiness at the 2026 Air and Space Forces Association Warfare Symposium in Aurora, Colorado, Feb. 24.
  • U.S. Coast Guard accepts delivery of 62nd Fast Response Cutter named for 9/11 hero US Coast Guard 26 Feb 2026 -- The U.S. Coast Guard accepted delivery of the 62nd Fast Response Cutter, USCGC Vincent Danz (WPC 1162), on Thursday in Key West.
  • AUKUS Submarine Maintenance Period Demonstrates Forward Sustainment in Australia US Navy 26 Feb 2026 -- Last November, on the edge of the Indian Ocean, just beyond mainland Australia and across the Garden Island Causeway to HMAS Stirling, a U.S. Virginia-class submarine quietly completed the first submarine maintenance period without the support of a U.S. submarine tender—a specialized vessel that provides mobile repair and supply services.
  • Task Force Ashland, USS Ashland arrive in Thailand for Exercise Cobra Gold 2026 US Navy 26 Feb 2026 -- Task Force (TF) Ashland, a forward-deployed force of U.S. Marines and Sailors aboard Whidbey Island-class dock landing ship USS Ashland (LSD 48), arrived in the Kingdom of Thailand, Feb. 22, to join approximately 8,000 service members from 30 nations for the 45th iteration of Exercise Cobra Gold, which runs from Feb. 24 to March 6, 2026.
  • USS Gerald R. Ford Crew Demonstrates Resilience, Readiness During Extended Deployment US Navy Press Release 26 Feb 2026 -- More than eight months into an extended deployment, the Sailors of USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN 78) continue to demonstrate resilience, professionalism, and sustained morale while serving far from home.

  • Steadfast Dart 26 puts NATO Airpower at centre of rapid reinforcement NATO Allied Air Command 26 Feb 2026 -- NATO recently concluded Exercise Steadfast Dart 26, which launched on Jan. 15, 2026, putting Allied Airpower at the heart of this rapid-reinforcement exercise across Central Europe.
  • NATO explores the role of geospatial intelligence in strengthening deterrence and defence NATO 26 Feb 2026 -- On Thursday (26 February 2026), NATO HQ hosted the first "Spatial Edge: USGIF Day", bringing together experts from Allies, industry and academia to examine how geospatial intelligence (GEOINT) supports NATO's collective defence.
  • NATO ramps up development of Future Surveillance Capability NATO 26 Feb 2026 -- On Wednesday (25 February), Allies agreed to move to the next stage in NATO's ambitious project that will deliver its future-fit surveillance capabilities.

  • MILAN 2026: frigate Marshal Shaposhnikov completes exercise in India Russia MoD 26 Feb 2026 -- The MILAN 2026 international naval exercise ended in the Republic of India, in which the Russian Navy was represented by the frigate Marshal Shaposhnikov of the Pacific Fleet.
  • Russia's Pacific Fleet Shows Naval Prowess as MILAN 2026 Drills Wrap Up Sputnik 26 Feb 2026 -- The Indian Navy's maritime exercise MILAN 2026 culminated with a closing ceremony onboard India's aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, off the coast of Visakhapatnam.

  • More weapons, lower interest rates: defense industry has already received over UAH 6.6 billion in preferential loans Ukraine MOD 26 Feb 2026
  • NATO Secretary General and the Prime Minister of Lithuania discuss collective security and support for Ukraine NATO 26 Feb 2026 -- On Thursday (26 February 2026), Secretary General Mark Rutte met Prime Minister Inga Ruginienė at the NATO Headquarters in Brussels.
  • New drone factory opens in Suffolk to boost Ukraine's Armed Forces against Russia's war UK MOD 26 Feb 2026 -- Ukraine's Armed Forces will be able to better defend itself against Russia's brutal attacks.
  • SSU launches regional cyber centres to counter russian cyberattacks on critical infrastructure Security Service of Ukraine 26 Feb 2026

  • Chairman Mast Leads Letter Urging the Treasury Department to Block U.S. Capital from Fueling the Chinese Military U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Foreign Affairs 26 Feb 2026 -- Today, House Foreign Affairs Committee Chairman Brian Mast urged the Treasury Department to take additional action to ensure American capital and expertise do not strengthen China's military industrial base.
  • Chairman Crawford Joins Fellow Congressional National Security Leaders Urging the Treasury Department to Block U.S. Capital from Fueling the Chinese Military US House of Representatives Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence 26 Feb 2026 -- Today, House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence Chairman Rick Crawford (AR-01) joined House Foreign Affairs Committee Chairman Brian Mast in urging the Treasury Department to take additional action to ensure American capital and expertise do not strengthen China's military industrial base.
  • FM spokesperson slams US' hype of China conducting nuclear testing as unsubstantiated and entirely groundless Global Times 26 Feb 2026 -- The US accusation is unsubstantiated and entirely groundless, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said, in response to media inquiry on reports that Christopher Yeaw, the US Assistant Secretary of State for the Bureau of Arms Control and Nonproliferation, accused China of conducting nuclear testing, and said the US will return to testing "on an equal basis" with other countries. The Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty Organization said on its website that they detected two very small seismic events in China on June 22, 2020, but with this data alone, it is not possible to assess the cause of these events with confidence.

  • Aussies and Kiwis build operational edge Australia DOD 26 Feb 2026 -- Royal New Zealand Air Force (RNZAF) personnel from 40 Squadron are working shoulder-to-shoulder with their Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) counterparts during Exercise Trojan Compass, maintaining aircraft, sharing flightline duties and demonstrating trans-Tasman mateship in action.
  • People to people, navy to navy Australia DOD 26 Feb 2026 -- HMAS Warramunga has arrived in Visakhapatnam, India, to participate in India's International Fleet Review (IFR) and Exercise Milan.
  • Grab-and-go on the high seas Australia DOD 26 Feb 2026 -- Traversing hemispheres and sailing the seas is thirsty business for an Anzac-class frigate, but they don't always have time to pull into port to keep on the move.
  • Flight training redefines air power generation Australia DOD 26 Feb 2026 -- Air Force Training Group has launched an innovative pilot training trial at RAAF Base Point Cook, partnering with RMIT University Aviation Academy to explore new ways of delivering Initial Flying Training (IFT).
  • Sharpening skills in desert skies Australia DOD 26 Feb 2026 -- Royal Australia Air Force (RAAF) aviators recently sharpened their air combat skills at two premier air training exercises out of Nellis Air Force Base in Nevada, alongside fighter aircraft and personnel from the United States and United Kingdom.

  • New Zealand's Navy, Army and Air Force combine for successful South-West Pacific operation New Zealand Defence Force 26 Feb 2026 -- The Governor-General of New Zealand's visit to Tokelau, upgrade of critical monitoring equipment on Raoul Island, and a search and rescue operation near Tonga were among the achievements of the NZDF's extensive operation to the South-West Pacific.
  • Kiwi drones to boost Defence capability New Zealand Government 26 Feb 2026 -- Innovative Kiwi drones will be trialled by the New Zealand Defence Force (NZDF) to strengthen capability while growing local industry, Defence Minister Judith Collins announced today.

  • 4th Meeting of Joint Defence Cooperation Committee between India and Kenya Concluded India PIB 26 Feb 2026 -- The fourth meeting of the Joint Defence Cooperation Committee (JDCC) between India and Kenya was held in Nairobi, Kenyabetween 24th and 26th February 2026
  • CDS inaugurates two-day CBRN Conclave organised by DRDO in New Delhi India PIB 26 Feb 2026 -- Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) is organising Chemical, Biological, Radiological and Nuclear (CBRN) Conclave at Manekshaw Centre, New Delhi on February 26 & 27, 2026
  • Future Warfare Course 3.0 Concludes, Strengthening Strategic and Technological Readiness India PIB 26 Feb 2026 -- The third edition of the Future Warfare Course (FWC-03) culminated on February 25, 2026 at Manekshaw Centre, New Delhi
  • Raksha Rajya Mantri inaugurates Chungthang-Lachen Axis and Taram Chu Bridge in Sikkim India PIB 26 Feb 2026 -- Raksha Rajya Mantri Shri Sanjay Seth inaugurated Chungthang - Lachen axis and a 400 ft Bailey Suspension Taram Chu Bridge in North Sikkim restored by the Border Roads Organisation (BRO)

  • Israeli army chief privately warns of cost of new war with Iran: Report Press TV 26 Feb 2026 -- Israel's army chief Eyal Zamir has privately warned about the devastating consequences of a potential war with Iran as an increased US military presence in the region raises the risk of a multi-front escalation.
  • Israel MOD Signs Approx $42 Million Deal with Ashot Ashkelon for Rehabilitation of Merkava Tank Transmissions Israel MOD 26 Feb 2026 -- The Defense Procurement Directorate (DPD) within the Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD) announced today (Thursday) the signing of an agreement with Ashot Ashkelon for the rehabilitation and supply of transmissions for Merkava tanks.

  • Iran's rejection of nuclear weapons based on religious beliefs, fatwa: Pezeshkian Press TV 26 Feb 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian has reiterated Iran's opposition to nuclear weapons, saying the policy is rooted in "theological" beliefs and a religious decree issued by Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei banning nuclear arms.
  • Iran Has No Plans Regarding Nuclear Weapons - President Sputnik 26 Feb 2026 -- Iran's leadership has no plans to obtain nuclear weapons, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said on Thursday, commenting on the US's remark on the absence of the relevant promise.

  • 'Fewer national wish lists': EDA conference debates defence industry support European Defence Agency 26 Feb 2026 -- The European Union must build a more responsive defence industry to assume greater responsibility for its own security and sustain support for Ukraine, the annual EDA industry conference heard, as pressure from the United States intensifies and Russia's illegal war of aggression in Ukraine enters its fifth year.

  • Jordan - Ku Band Multi-Function Radio Frequency System (KuMRFS) Radars US Dept. of State 26 Feb 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Jordan of Ku band multi-function radio frequency system (KuMRFS) radars and related equipment for an estimated cost of $280 million.

  • Strengthening drone defence Germany - Federal Government 26 Feb 2026 -- The Federal Government plans to improve drone defence. Relevant amendments to the Aviation Security Act were passed in the German Bundestag. The key changes at a glance.

  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Greek Foreign Minister Gerapetritis US Dept. of State 26 Feb 2026
  • OSINT Subcommittee Member Rep. Perry Joins Recorded Future's Public Sector Intelligence Forum US House of Representatives Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence 26 Feb 2026

  • ICYMI: HFAC Joins Appropriators in Delivering Joint Report on Christian Persecution in Nigeria to the White House U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Foreign Affairs 26 Feb 2026 -- Following President Trump's call-to-action and redesignation of Nigeria as a Country of Particular Concern (CPC), the House Appropriations Committee and House Foreign Affairs Committee delivered a joint report on the persecution of Christians in Nigeria to the White House.

  • Treasury Proposes Rule to Sever Swiss Bank MBaer's Access to U.S. Financial System US Treasury 26 Feb 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) proposed a rule that, if finalized, would sever MBaer Merchant Bank AG (MBaer's) access to the U.S. financial system as a result of its financial support to illicit actors linked to Russia and Iran. If finalized, the proposed rule would prohibit covered U.S. financial institutions from opening or maintaining a correspondent account for, or on behalf of, MBaer.

  • Palestinian factions denounce US for offering 'consular services' inside West Bank settlement Press TV 26 Feb 2026 -- Several Palestinian resistance factions have unanimously condemned the decision by the United States embassy in the occupied territories to provide "consular services" inside an illegal settlement in the occupied West Bank.

  • Palestine: UN rights chief highlights suffering, atrocity crimes 'that remain unpunished' UN News 26 Feb 2026 -- The UN rights chief Volker Türk on Thursday highlighted the "human-made disaster" across the Occupied Palestinian Territory stemming from Israel's disregard for human rights norms and serious violations also committed by Hamas and other Palestinian armed groups.

  • COMMUNIQUE ADOPTED BY THE EMERGENCY OPEN-ENDED MEETING OF THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 26 Feb 2026 -- The Emergency Open-Ended Meeting of the Executive Committee of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) at the level of Foreign Ministers, held at the OIC General Secretariat headquarters in Jeddah on February 26, 2026 (corresponding to Ramadan 9, 1447 AH), at the request of the State of Palestine, to address the announcement by Israel, the occupying power, to impose an illegal reality, expand colonial settlement and deepen annexation and seizure policies in order to alter the status and character of the occupied Palestinian territory in the West Bank, including Al-Quds Al-Shareef
  • Chair's Summary of the Outcome Of The Emergency Open-Ended Meeting of the Executive Committee of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) at the level of Foreign Ministers, held on February 26, 2026. Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 26 Feb 2026 -- Motivated by the Charter and resolutions of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC), the meeting emphasized the centrality of the Palestinian cause and the holy city of Al-Quds Al-Sharif to the entire Islamic Ummah, and reaffirmed the political, legal, historical, and moral responsibility to stand in full solidarity with the Palestinian people
  • The Executive Committee of the OIC at the level of Foreign Ministers meets to address the decisions of the Israeli occupation authorities aimed at promoting settlement and annexing the West Bank Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 26 Feb 2026 -- The Executive Committee of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation held an extraordinary meeting, open to all Members, at the level of Foreign Ministers of the Organisation's Member States, today, Thursday, 26 February 2026, at the OIC General Secretariat headquarters in Jeddah to discuss the illegal decisions of the Israeli occupation aimed at promoting settlement and annexation and attempting to impose alleged Israeli sovereignty over the occupied West Bank
  • With the participation of Foreign Ministers of Member States, the OIC holds an Emergency Meeting to confront Israeli annexation plans Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 26 Feb 2026 -- The Executive Committee meeting at the level of Foreign Ministers kicked off today at the headquarters of the OIC General Secretariat in a high-level diplomatic move to discuss the serious repercussions of the Israeli occupation's measures aimed at annexing parts of the occupied West Bank
  • Statement Of The DPM/FM At The Extraordinary Open-Ended Ministerial Session Of The OIC Executive Committee (26th February 2026, Jeddah) Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Feb 2026
  • HRC 61 - Interactive Dialogue on High Commissioner's report on Occupied Palestinian Territory - EU Statement European External Action Service (EEAS) 26 Feb 2026
  • Russian Foreign Ministry Still Drafting Stance on Participation in Board of Peace - Kremlin Sputnik 26 Feb 2026 -- The Russian Foreign Ministry continues to formulate a position on the expediency of the country's participation in the Board of Peace, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Thursday.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Ukraine UNOCHA 26 Feb 2026

  • AFP monitoring Scarborough Shoal for reclamation activities Philippine News Agency 26 Feb 2026 -- A ranking naval official said the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) is using all means possible to monitor Scarborough Shoal (also known as Bajo de Masinloc) in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) to ensure that no illegal or reclamation activities are taking place in the vital waterway.

  • Pakistan Continues To Strike Taliban Targets In Afghanistan Amid Signals Of 'Open War' RFE/RL 26 Feb 2026 -- Pakistani war planes continued to bomb targets in Afghanistan on February 27 as fears of all out war between the two rose as they trade strikes on each other in their volatile mountain border region following days of escalating tensions boiled over.
  • Explosions Rock Kabul After Pakistan And Afghanistan Trade Strikes In Volatile Border Region RFE/RL 26 Feb 2026 -- Several explosions have rocked Kabul just hours after Afghanistan and Pakistan traded strikes on each other in their volatile mountain border region as days of escalating tensions boiled over with both sides claiming to have killed dozens of militants and security forces.
  • Afghanistan Launches 'Retaliatory Operation' Against Pakistan - Military Official Sputnik 26 Feb 2026 -- The Afghan armed forces have launched an operation in response to Pakistani air force bombings on their territory, a spokesman for Afghanistan's 201st Khalid bin Walid Army Corps said on Thursday.
  • UN's Türk urges dialogue after deadly clashes on Afghan-Pakistan border UN News 26 Feb 2026 -- UN human rights chief Volker Türk on Thursday appealed for dialogue between Afghanistan and Pakistan amid border clashes and deadly airstrikes, while condemning ever harsher "apartheid" edicts issued by the Afghan de facto authorities that continue to severely impact women and girls.

  • HRC61 - Enhanced interactive dialogue on the report of the Special Rapporteur on Afghanistan and the OHCHR report - EU Statement European External Action Service (EEAS) 26 Feb 2026
  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK Statement for the Enhanced Interactive Dialogue on Afghanistan UK FCDO 26 Feb 2026 -- UK Statement for the Enhanced Interactive Dialogue on Afghanistan. Delivered by the UK's Human Rights Ambassador, Eleanor Sanders.

  • Further to an intelligence based operation in Zhob District of Balochistan on 24 February 2026, in which eight Indian sponsored Khwarij were successfully neutralized; on 25 February 2026, security forces conducted a comprehensive area sanitization operation in Zhob District of Balochistan, to hunt down hiding Khwarij belonging to Indian proxy, Fitna al Khwarij. ISPR Pakistan 26 Feb 2026

  • Border guards seize cache of weapons, ammo in southeast Iran after ambushing terrorists Press TV 26 Feb 2026 -- Iranian border guards seized a cache of weapons, ammunition, and explosive materials after ambushing terrorists in the southeast of the country, a senior border commander said.

  • Statement by the Spokesperson Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Feb 2026 -- In response to media queries regarding the 19th Anniversary of the Samjhota Express Terror Attack, the the Spokesperson Mr. Tahir Andrabi states the following:

  • Sudan: UN expert calls for more support to Emergency Response Rooms volunteers OHCHR 26 Feb 2026 -- As war continues to ravage Sudan with horrific consequences, local Emergency Response Rooms volunteers continue to risk their lives to deliver life-saving aid across the country, a UN expert said today.
  • Human Rights Council hears of ongoing risk of further genocidal violence in Sudan UN News 26 Feb 2026 -- Nearly three years of war in Sudan have been marked by killings, rape and other violations, with risk of genocidal violence spreading, the UN Human Rights Council heard on Thursday.
  • HRC61 - Enhanced interactive dialogue on the report of the FFM and the report of the High Commissioner on the Sudan - EU statement European External Action Service (EEAS) 26 Feb 2026
  • UN Human Rights Council 61: UK Statement for the Enhanced Interactive Dialogue on Sudan UK FCDO 26 Feb 2026 -- UK Statement for the Enhanced Interactive Dialogue on Sudan. Delivered by the UK's Permanent Representative to the WTO and UN, Kumar Iyer.
  • UN Fact Finding Mission report on El Fasher: Sudan Core Group foreign ministers' statement UK FCDO 26 Feb 2026 -- Statement from the foreign ministers of the Sudan Core Group on the UN Fact Finding Mission (FFM) report on El Fasher.
  • Statement on the UN Fact Finding Mission report on El Fasher Government of Norway 26 Feb 2026 -- Statement from the foreign ministers of the Sudan Core Group on the UN Fact Finding Mission (FFM) report on El Fasher.

  • DRC: UN experts warn of extreme M23 violence targeting human rights defenders OHCHR 26 Feb 2026 -- UN experts* today condemned the extreme violence committed against human rights defenders by the M23 armed group in the North and South Kivu provinces, in eastern DRC.

News Reports

  • Sinaloa Cartel Leader Charged with Narcoterrorism, Material Support of Terrorism and Drug Trafficking US Dept. of Justice 26 Feb 2026 -- A superseding indictment unsealed today charges René Arzate-García, also known as "La Rana," alleged Tijuana plaza boss for the Sinaloa Cartel, with Narcoterrorism and Material Support of Terrorism in connection with trafficking massive amounts of fentanyl, cocaine, methamphetamine and marijuana into the United States.
  • Up to $10 Million in Reward Offers for Information Leading to Arrests and/or Convictions of Alleged Mexican Sinaloa Cartel Plaza Bosses US Dept. of State 26 Feb 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of State's Bureau of International Narcotics and Law Enforcement Affairs is announcing reward offers under the Narcotics Rewards Program (NRP) of up to $5 million each for information leading to the arrests and/or convictions, in any country, of Sinaloa Cartel Tijuana Plaza Bosses René Arzate-García, a/k/a "La Rana," and his brother, Alfonso Arzate-García, a/k/a "Aquiles."

  • ProtectEU: Commission presents new counterterrorism agenda European Commission 26 Feb 2026 -- Today, the European Commission presented a new agenda to prevent and counter terrorism, setting out the way forward to reinforce Europe's collective response to evolving terrorist and violent extremist threats.

  • UN drug alert stops shipment that could have made 1.6 billion lethal fentanyl doses UN News 26 Feb 2026 -- An international early warning system blocked a shipment of chemicals used to make fentanyl that could have produced up to 1.6 billion potentially lethal doses, the UN narcotics control body said on Thursday.

  • Government receives final COVID Inquiry report New Zealand Government 26 Feb 2026 -- The Government welcomes the delivery of the final report from the Royal Commission of Inquiry into COVID-19 Lessons to the Governor-General this morning.

  • Statement by the Deputy Foreign Minister of the Republic of Cuba, Carlos Fernández de Cossío Cuba Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Feb 2026 -- "Yesterday, our authorities reported an attempted infiltration for terrorist purposes involving 10 individuals aboard a vessel registered in the State of Florida, United States."
  • Russia Slams Boat Incident Off Cuba as US Aggressive Provocation Sputnik 26 Feb 2026 -- The Cuban Interior Ministry has stated that a US-flagged boat entered Cuban waters and opened fire, and Cuban border guards returned fire and killed four people aboard the vessel.
  • Humanitarian pressures grow as Cuba continues to struggle with energy shortages UN News 26 Feb 2026 -- Cuba's humanitarian situation is worsening as fuel shortages deepen nearly a month after Washington took measures to block oil supplies from entering the Caribbean nation, a senior UN official warned on Wednesday.

  • Russia's FSB Prevents Terrorist Attack Against Senior Military Officer in St. Petersburg Sputnik 26 Feb 2026 -- A terrorist attack against a high-ranking military officer of the Russian Defense Ministry was prevented in St. Petersburg, and two Russians who acted in the interests of the special services of Ukraine were detained, Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB) told Sputnik.
  • Meeting of the Supreme State Council of the Union State President of Russia 26 Feb 2026 -- Vladimir Putin and President of Belarus Alexander Lukashenko held a meeting of the Supreme State Council of the Union State in the Grand Kremlin Palace.
  • Statement of the Permanent Representative of the Russian Federation to the United Nations Office and Other International Organizations in Geneva Ambassador Gennady Gatilov at the High-Level Segment of the Conference on Disarmament in the exercise of the right of reply, Geneva, February 25, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Feb 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's answers to media questions, Moscow, February 26, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Feb 2026
  • Russia, Belarus Team up to Resist Sanctions, Cooperate on Foreign Policy & Defense Sputnik 26 Feb 2026 -- The process of deepening the integration of Russia and Belarus is natural and mutually beneficial, Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Thursday.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Mao Ning's Regular Press Conference on February 26, 2026 PRC MofA 26 Feb 2026
  • China placing relevant Japanese entities on export control and watch lists is entirely justified, reasonable, and lawful: MOFCOM Global Times 26 Feb 2026 -- When responding to question on China placing Japanese entities on export control and watch lists, He Yongqian, spokesperson of Ministry of Commerce, said on Thursday that China's lawful listing action targets only a small number of Japanese entities and applies solely to dual-use items. "These measures do not affect normal economic and trade exchanges between China and Japan. Law-abiding and compliant Japanese entities have no cause for concern," she said.
  • Xi urges good start to 15th Five-Year Plan period Global Times 26 Feb 2026 -- Xi Jinping, general secretary of the Communist Party of China (CPC) Central Committee, asked senior Party officials to ensure a good start to the 15th Five-Year Plan period (2026-2030).
  • "We want a partnership with China" Germany - Federal Government 26 Feb 2026 -- It is his first visit to the People's Republic while in office: Federal Chancellor Merz has travelled to China. In Beijing, he was received for talks by Premier Li Qiang and President Xi Jinping. The Chancellor also visited German companies in Hangzhou.
  • China, Germany agree to deepen partnership, win-win cooperation in Xi-Merz meeting PRC MND 26 Feb 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping met with German Chancellor Friedrich Merz in Beijing on Wednesday, with both sides agreeing to promote the continuous new development of the all-round strategic partnership, promote dialogue and cooperation, and uphold multilateralism and free trade.
  • Jimmy Lai reportedly wins appeal in separate fraud case, still serving 20-year sentence for national security offenses Global Times 26 Feb 2026 -- Jimmy Lai, founder of Next Digital, has had his fraud conviction in a Hong Kong Science Park-related case overturned on appeal. Lai is serving 20-year sentence in a separate national security case, according to local media reports on Thursday.

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 26 Feb 2026

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un's Speech at Military Parade Held in Commemoration of Ninth Congress of WPK KCNA 26 Feb 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un made a speech at the military parade held in commemoration of the Ninth Congress of the Workers' Party of Korea.
  • Let Us Make Great Victory and Glory Gained through Indomitable Pioneering Struggle Lead onto Steady Prosperity and Leaps Forward in New Struggle Report on Ninth Congress of Workers' Party of Korea KCNA 26 Feb 2026 -- The Workers' Party of Korea, the vanguard of the Korean revolution, is ushering in a new era of building a powerful nation by leading tens of millions of Korean people. Its sacred mission, strong pride and matchless fighting efficiency are bringing about a radical turn in the position and destiny of our country and people and placing the great strength, high dignity and eye-opening prosperity desired by all generations on this land on the majesty of history.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un's Closing Address at Ninth WPK Congress KCNA 26 Feb 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un made a closing address at the Ninth Congress of the Workers' Party of Korea.
  • Military Parade Held with Splendor in Commemoration of Ninth WPK Congress KCNA 26 Feb 2026 -- In the watershed where a new era of building a powerful and prosperous nation is being ushered in on the road of the Juche revolution, the heroic Korean People's Army celebrating the Ninth Congress of the Workers' Party of Korea as the great one of victory and glory has proudly stood on the starting line of the revolutionary journey, in which new grandeur and prosperity are in store, with absolute trust and confidence in the goal of new victory set by our Party.
  • First Politburo Meeting of Ninth C.C., WPK Held KCNA 26 Feb 2026 -- The First Meeting of the Political Bureau of the Ninth Central Committee of the Workers' Party of Korea was held at the headquarters building of the Party Central Committee on Feb. 25.
  • North Korea Open to Rapprochement If US Respects Its Nuclear Status - Kim Jong-un Sputnik 26 Feb 2026 -- North Korean leader Kim Jong-un said that Pyongyang has no reason not to pursue rapprochement with the United States if Washington abandons hostility and respects North Korea's nuclear status.
  • Kim Jong-un Vows 'Terrible' Retaliation for Any Military Acts Against North Korea Sputnik 26 Feb 2026 -- North Korean leader Kim Jong-un has declared the country's military readiness to deliver merciless retaliation in response to any hostile military actions against the country.

  • 'Good progress' made in 'most serious and longest' Iran-US talks: FM Araghchi IRNA 26 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the Iranian and US delegations have made "good progress" in the third round of indirect negotiations held in the Swiss city of Geneva.
  • Iran, US move 'closer to agreement' on some issues after 'serious, longest' round of talks: FM Press TV 26 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the latest round of indirect talks with the United States was one of the best, most serious, and longest to date, and that Tehran and Washington moved "closer to an agreement" on some issues.
  • Omani FM Cites 'Significant Progress' As US, Iran Conclude Fresh Round Of Nuclear Talks RFE/RL 26 Feb 2026 -- US and Iranian officials held a fresh round of Oman-mediated nuclear talks in Geneva on February 26 for what is seen as a last-ditch attempt to avoid a major military conflict between the foes.
  • Indirect nuclear talks with US continuing 'seriously' in Geneva, FM spox says IRNA 26 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei says that indirect nuclear talks between Iran and the US are continuing seriously in Geneva, Switzerland.
  • 'Creative and positive ideas' exchanged in Iran-US talks in Geneva: Omani FM IRNA 26 Feb 2026 -- Oman's Foreign Minister, Sayyid Badr al-Busaidi, says that creative and positive ideas were exchanged between the Iranian and US delegations in Geneva.
  • Iran says it's serious in nuclear talks with US as third round begins in Geneva IRNA 26 Feb 2026 -- Representatives from Iran and the United States have begun the third round of indirect nuclear negotiations in Geneva, Switzerland.
  • US-Iran technical talks set for Vienna next week after 'significant progress' in Geneva Press TV 26 Feb 2026 -- Technical-level talks between the United States and Iran will be held next week in Vienna after what Oman's foreign minister described as significant progress in the latest round of negotiations, he said on Thursday.
  • 3rd round of nuclear talks opens in Geneva; Iran's proposal seen as 'test' of US sincerity Press TV 26 Feb 2026 -- The Iranian delegation to the third round of indirect nuclear talks with the United States has reportedly put forward a proposal that would eliminate all of Washington's pretexts regarding Iran's peaceful nuclear program.
  • Pezeshkian rejects Trump's claims on Iran unrest casualties IRNA 26 Feb 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian has dismissed claims by US President Donald Trump regarding casualty figures from recent unrest in Iran, saying that any doubts about Tehran's official data must be supported by evidence.
  • Iran Armed Forces warn US: 'Any unwise action will ignite a widespread fire' in West Asia Press TV 26 Feb 2026 -- Brigadier General Abolfazl Shekarchi, spokesman for the Iranian Armed Forces, has rejected the recent statements of US President Donald Trump as "baseless, boastful, and delusional" and warned of severe consequences for any aggression against Iran.
  • IRGC official: US buildup, psychological tactics aim to 'swallow Iran again' Press TV 26 Feb 2026 -- Iranian military commanders have slammed the enemies for waging psychological and cognitive warfare to destabilize the country, targeting national security, public morale, and economic stability.
  • Chinese FM responds to question of whether China will support Iran alongside Russia if the US attacks Iran Global Times 26 Feb 2026 -- China is closely following the development of the situation in Iran. China consistently advocates for resolving issues through political and diplomatic means, and opposes the use or threat of force in international relations, said Chinese FM spokesperson Mao Ning at Thursday's press briefing, in response to a question that if the US launches an attack on Iran, will China support Iran alongside Russia?
  • West pursuing its past policy of 'plundering' Iran's oil: Russia IRNA 26 Feb 2026 -- Russia has accused Western powers of pursuing policies towards the Islamic Republic of Iran with aim of exploiting the country's resources, especially its oil fields, as they did in the past.
  • Russia: West seeks to repeat past 'plunder' of Iran's oil Press TV 26 Feb 2026 -- Moscow has slammed Western powers for pursuing policies toward Iran that mirror historical attempts to exploit the country's resources.
  • US, Iran To Meet For Nuclear Talks Amid Fears Of Military Conflict RFE/RL 26 Feb 2026 -- US and Iranian officials are set to hold a fresh round of nuclear talks in Geneva on February 26 for what is seen as a last-ditch attempt to avoid a major military conflict between the foes.
  • Iran, U.S. signal openness to diplomacy as nuclear talks approach People's Daily 26 Feb 2026 -- Iran expressed optimism on Wednesday over the upcoming nuclear talks with the United States, as both sides signaled a willingness to pursue diplomacy amid heightened regional tensions.
  • U.S. imposes new sanctions targeting Iran's oil sales, weapons production People's Daily 26 Feb 2026 -- The U.S. administration on Wednesday sanctioned over 30 individuals, entities and vessels enabling "illicit" Iran's oil sales and ballistic missile and advanced conventional weapons (ACW) production.

  • Iraqi anti-terror groups threaten action if US continues occupation, interference Press TV 26 Feb 2026 -- The Iraqi Resistance Coordination Committee, an umbrella of six anti-terror groups, has warned that it will take action if the United States keeps occupying the country and dictating its will on the nation.

  • PM Netanyahu's statement together with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi Israel MFA 26 Feb 2026 -- The future belongs to those who innovate, and Israel and India are bent on innovation. We are proud ancient civilizations, very proud of our past, but also absolutely determined to seize our future and we can do it better together.
  • President Herzog welcomes Indian PM Narendra Modi to the President's Residence in Jerusalem Israel MFA 26 Feb 2026 -- President Herzog: "India is an essential part of the great future of the Middle East, and the Middle East is an essential part of the great future of India"
  • PM Netanyahu's Statement Together with Indian PM Narendra Modi Israel - Prime Minister 26 Feb 2026
  • Prime Minister meets with the President of Israel India PIB 26 Feb 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi today called on the President of Israel, H
  • Prime Minister meets with Prime Minister of Israel India PIB 26 Feb 2026 -- On his State Visit to Israel, Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held official talks with the Prime Minister of Israel, H.E. Mr. Benjamin Netanyahu, in Jerusalem on February 26, 2026.
  • India - Israel Joint Statement India PIB 26 Feb 2026
  • List of Outcomes: Visit of Prime Minister to Israel India PIB 26 Feb 2026
  • English Translation of Prime Minister's Press Statement during the Joint Press Statement with Prime Minister of Israel India PIB 26 Feb 2026

  • Transcript of the Weekly Press Briefing by the Spokesperson on Thursday, 26th February 2026 Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 26 Feb 2026

  • Council of Europe committed to continuing cooperation with Belarusian democratic forces and civil society Council of Europe 26 Feb 2026 -- The Secretary General of the Council of Europe, Alain Berset, in a meeting in Strasbourg with the leader of the Belarusian democratic forces, Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya, on the margins of her address to the Committee of Ministers, reaffirmed the commitment of the Council of Europe to address the aspiration for a free and democratic Belarus in a dynamic and challenging international environment, notably through the work of its Contact Group on Belarus.
  • European Union extends sanctions against Belarus Lithuania MFA 26 Feb 2026 -- On 26 February, the Council of the European Union decided to extend the sanctions against Belarus for another year. The restrictive measures will remain in force to the same extent as until now, with no changes.

  • Cambodia - Meeting between Jean-Noel Barrot and Mr Prak Sokhonn, Minister of Foreign affairs and International Cooperation of Cambodia (26.02.26) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 26 Feb 2026 -- Jean-Noël Barrot, Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs, had a meeting on Wednesday 25 February with his counterpart Mr Prak Sokhonn, Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation of Cambodia.

  • PM call with Crown Prince of Saudi Arabia, His Royal Highness Mohammed bin Salman: 26 February 2026 Gov.UK 26 Feb 2026

  • Poland Reaffirms Support for Armenia's European Path Republic of Poland 26 Feb 2026 -- "Armenia's European aspirations are absolutely justified. Poland was, is, and will remain an advocate of wise and pragmatic enlargement of the European Union," Prime Minister Donald Tusk said after meeting with Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan.

  • The Prime Minister meets with Polish President Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 26 Feb 2026
  • Nikol Pashinyan and Donald Tusk discuss a number of issues related to the development of multi-sectoral cooperation between Armenia and Poland Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 26 Feb 2026

  • Cambodia: EU leaders receive Prime Minister of Cambodia Hun Manet strengthening EU-Cambodia Ties European External Action Service (EEAS) 26 Feb 2026 -- Prime Minister of Cambodia Samdech Moha Borvor Thipadei Hun Manet visited the European Union Institutions on 24 and 25 February 2026, marking an important step in the EU-Cambodia relations and aiming to further deepen bilateral cooperation.

  • EU Sanctions Envoy Visits Bishkek Amid Concerns Over Russian Circumvention RFE/RL 26 Feb 2026 -- European Union sanctions envoy David O'Sullivan arrived in Bishkek for high-level meetings with Kyrgyz officials amid concerns that some financial channels and trade flows in Kyrgyzstan are being used to circumvent sanctions against Russia.

  • Presentation by the President of the Committee of Ministers of the Council of Europe: UK statement to the OSCE UK FCDO 26 Feb 2026 -- Ambassador Holland commends Deputy Prime Minister Mihail Popșoi for Moldova's democratic reforms and resilience in the face of Russian hybrid threats, emphasising the UK's continued support for its European integration. He highlights the complementary roles of the OSCE and Council of Europe and underscores the need for accountability for Russia's aggression against Ukraine.

  • Treaty on Gibraltar published, ushering in new era of coexistence and shared prosperity Spain MFA 26 Feb 2026 -- The agreement removes the border fence, guarantees the free movement of people and goods, and establishes a framework for fiscal, environmental and social cooperation, while maintaining Spain's position on sovereignty intact

  • Orban Asks EU to Organize Mission to Check Condition of Druzhba Pipeline Sputnik 26 Feb 2026 -- Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban has asked the EU to organize a mission to check the condition of the Druzhba oil pipeline, through which Ukraine stopped pumping oil, according to his letter to European Council President Antonio Costa on Thursday.

  • Sanctioning the Murillo-Ortega Dictatorship's Agents Enforcing Its Repression of the Nicaraguan People US Dept. of State 26 Feb 2026 -- The Trump Administration is acting once again to hold Nicaraguan dictators Rosario María Murillo Zambrana and Daniel Ortega Saavedra and their enablers to account.
  • Treasury Sanctions Nicaraguan Officials Enabling the Murillo-Ortega Dictatorship's Repression US Treasury 26 Feb 2026 -- Today, the Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned five Nicaraguan government officials who lead the principal financial, communications, and military agencies that enable Nicaragua's Murillo-Ortega dictatorship to repress its people. The individuals sanctioned include the Director and Deputy Director of Nicaragua's Financial Analysis Unit, the Minister of Labor, the Deputy Director General of the Nicaraguan Institute of Telecommunications and Postal Services, and the head of the Nicaraguan Army's Directorate of Military Intelligence and Counterintelligence.

  • World News in Brief: Russian strikes on Ukraine condemned, migrant deaths update, devastating floods in southern Brazil UN News 26 Feb 2026 -- The UN on Thursday condemned the latest attacks against civilians in Ukraine by Russian forces, reiterating that they always constitute a violation of international law "and must stop immediately."
